why.keep_all: ratified keep-all under the cull policy: 147/147 registered; the two z>=3.5 flags are artifacts of an ultra-tight distribution (FWHM 3.72-3.80, ~2% total spread, so MAD is tiny) — DSC_8485 flags at fwhm z 3.47 / round z 3.62 but its ABSOLUTE deviation is +1.2% FWHM (3.80 vs median 3.765), nowhere near the frame-wide wreck class (set-04's DSC_7944 precedent was z +14); DSC_8372 is a single mild nstars dip (z 3.57), everything else normal. Dropping a minority subset buys no matching gain and pays the sqrt(N) noise penalty (dead-end registry).
why.satellites_kept: 3 satellite objects across 20 frame-detections (audit_work/anomaly_audit.json), no aircraft, no unknowns — moving minority trails stack clean through in-group rejection; user-ratified 'satellites stay'.
why.session_edges_kept: DSC_8341 (first) and DSC_8487 (last) are QA-clean and not anomaly-flagged — no session-edge cull.
why.weight_null: wFWHM weighting at this set's ~2% FWHM spread is worse than none (min-max ramp soft-culls the worst frame at any spread).
